what: Live end-to-end measurement of the bus-runtime integration (PHASE_BUS_RUNTIME_1): the SAME work (5 context-warden smoke-runs) via fresh subprocess fan-out vs one warm bus worker. Turns the efficiency PROJECTION into a measured end-to-end number on the real Rust lewis-aos-bus daemon.
method: node bench/bus-e2e/measure.mjs context-warden 5 — fresh arm = N `agix agent smoke` subprocess spawns; warm arm = one `agix agent serve` worker + N bus dispatches. Wall-clock from the harness.

result.interpretation: Fresh fan-out re-pays the full process+runtime+agent load (~530ms) PER task; the warm bus worker pays it ONCE (513ms) then serves each task in ~5ms (the bus hop + the smoke work). The AOS analog of the 75K-token per-spawn tax, now measured end-to-end over the real bus.

honest_caveats: Model-free (smoke op) → measures process/runtime SETUP overhead, NOT tokens. It is the AOS-runtime analog of the token tax, not the token tax itself.
Warm amortizes one 513ms startup over N tasks: no win at N=1; the win grows with N (the fan-out case). At N=5 the break-even is already passed (warm total 536ms vs fresh 2651ms).

Per-task warm (~5ms) still includes a fresh orchestrator bus connection per dispatch (the fanout primitive reconnects each call) — a connection-reuse optimization would lower it further toward the ~8.6us transport floor.
Single run, one machine. Re-run: node bench/bus-e2e/measure.mjs [agent] [N].
